title: 30-Day Strength Foundation
description: Build a solid strength base with progressive overload. Perfect for beginners moving into intermediate training.

days.title: Assess Your Starting Point
days.exercise_description: Today you'll establish your baseline. Perform each movement with bodyweight only: squats, push-ups, lunges, and planks. Record your max reps and hold times. This isn't about pushing limits — it's about understanding where you are so we can track real progress.
days.coach_tip: Don't ego-lift on day one. Honest numbers today mean visible gains by week four.

days.title: Squat Mechanics
days.exercise_description: Focus entirely on squat form. Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out. Descend until thighs are parallel. We'll do 5 sets of 8 with controlled tempo — 3 seconds down, 1 second pause, 2 seconds up.
days.coach_tip: Film your last set from the side. Compare it to the demo. Small form fixes now prevent injuries later.

days.title: Upper Body Push
days.exercise_description: Push-up variations progressing from knees to full to decline. Shoulder press with dumbbells or household items. 4 sets of 10 for each movement. Rest 60 seconds between sets.
days.coach_tip: If you can do more than 12 reps easily, increase resistance. The last 2 reps should feel genuinely challenging.

days.title: Hinge & Pull Patterns
days.exercise_description: Romanian deadlifts, bent-over rows, and face pulls. These posterior chain movements counterbalance all the pushing. 4 sets of 8 with slow eccentrics.
days.coach_tip: Think about squeezing your shoulder blades together on every pull. The mind-muscle connection matters more than the weight.

days.title: Core & Stability
days.exercise_description: Dead bugs, pallof press, farmer's carries, and hollow holds. Core training isn't crunches — it's about resisting movement under load. 3 sets each, 30-45 seconds per set.
days.coach_tip: If your lower back arches during dead bugs, you've gone too far. Reduce range of motion and own it.

days.title: Full Body Integration
days.exercise_description: Combine this week's movements into a circuit: squats, push-ups, rows, deadlifts, planks. 3 rounds, 10 reps each, minimal rest between exercises, 2 minutes between rounds.
days.coach_tip: This is about flow, not failure. Keep form tight even when fatigue builds. Sloppy reps teach sloppy patterns.

days.title: Active Recovery & Reassess
days.exercise_description: Light stretching, foam rolling, and a gentle 20-minute walk. Then repeat the Day 1 assessment. Compare your numbers — most people see 10-15% improvement in just one week.
days.coach_tip: Recovery is when your body actually gets stronger. Skipping rest days is not dedication — it's impatience.

title: Explosive Power Training
description: Develop speed and power through plyometrics and Olympic lift progressions. For intermediate athletes.

days.title: Jump Assessment
days.exercise_description: Measure your standing broad jump, vertical jump, and 10-meter sprint. These baselines will track your power development over the program.
days.coach_tip: Warm up thoroughly before any maximal effort. Cold muscles and explosive movements are a recipe for injury.

days.title: Box Jumps & Depth Drops
days.exercise_description: Start with low box jumps (12-16 inches). Focus on soft landings — balls of feet first, knees tracking over toes. Progress to depth drops from the box. 5 sets of 5 with full recovery between sets.
days.coach_tip: Power training requires full rest between sets. This isn't cardio — 2-3 minutes between sets is appropriate.

days.title: Medicine Ball Throws
days.exercise_description: Overhead throws, chest passes, rotational throws, and slams. Each movement trains power in a different plane. 4 sets of 6 for each variation.
days.coach_tip: Release the ball at peak velocity. Deceleration is wasted energy — throw like you mean it.

days.title: Sprint Mechanics
days.exercise_description: A-skips, B-skips, butt kicks at increasing intensity. Then 6x30m sprints at 85-90% effort with walk-back recovery.
days.coach_tip: Sprinting is a skill, not just running fast. Focus on driving your knees forward and striking the ground under your center of mass.

days.title: Power Complex
days.exercise_description: Pair a strength move with an explosive move: squat + jump squat, bench press + plyometric push-up, deadlift + broad jump. 4 pairs, 5 reps each, 3 sets.
days.coach_tip: The strength move activates the muscle fibers, and the explosive move teaches them to fire faster. This is called post-activation potentiation.

title: Morning Cardio Kickstart
description: Wake up with 20-minute high-energy sessions designed for before work. No equipment needed.

days.title: Jumping Jack Intervals
days.exercise_description: Alternating 40 seconds of jumping jacks with 20 seconds of rest. 10 rounds. Simple, effective, and gets your heart rate up fast.
days.coach_tip: Land softly on the balls of your feet. Your downstairs neighbors will thank you.

days.title: Burpee Ladder
days.exercise_description: 1 burpee, rest 10 seconds. 2 burpees, rest 10 seconds. Continue up to 10, then back down to 1. Total: 100 burpees in about 18 minutes.
days.coach_tip: If full burpees are too much, step back instead of jumping back. Same cardio benefit, less joint stress.

days.title: Shadow Boxing
days.exercise_description: 3-minute rounds of shadow boxing with 1 minute rest. Jab, cross, hook, uppercut. Keep moving your feet. 5 rounds total.
days.coach_tip: Don't just wave your arms. Rotate your hips with each punch — that's where the power comes from.

days.title: High Knees & Mountain Climbers
days.exercise_description: 30 seconds high knees, 30 seconds mountain climbers, 30 seconds rest. 8 rounds. Focus on driving your knees as high and fast as possible.
days.coach_tip: Pump your arms during high knees — it forces your legs to move faster. Arms lead, legs follow.

days.title: Tabata Classic
days.exercise_description: 20 seconds max effort, 10 seconds rest, 8 rounds. Exercise: squat jumps. Then rest 2 minutes and repeat with push-ups. Total: 2 Tabata blocks.
days.coach_tip: True Tabata should leave you on the floor. If you can talk during the work intervals, you're not going hard enough.

days.title: Stair Repeats
days.exercise_description: Find a staircase (even 1 flight works). Sprint up, walk down. Repeat for 15 minutes. If no stairs, do step-ups on a sturdy chair.
days.coach_tip: Push off your entire foot, not just your toes. Engage your glutes on every step.

days.title: Dance Cardio Freestyle
days.exercise_description: Put on your favorite upbeat playlist and move for 20 minutes. No rules, no choreography. Just don't stop moving. Jump, twist, groove — whatever feels good.
days.coach_tip: Cardio doesn't have to feel like punishment. If you're smiling, you'll come back tomorrow. That's the real win.

title: Couch to 5K Running Plan
description: Go from zero running to completing 5 kilometers in 7 weeks. Walk-run intervals that build gradually.

days.title: Walk-Run Intro
days.exercise_description: Walk 5 minutes to warm up. Then alternate: run 60 seconds, walk 90 seconds. Repeat 8 times. Walk 5 minutes to cool down. Total: ~30 minutes.
days.coach_tip: Your running pace should be conversational — if you can't say a full sentence, slow down.

days.title: Building the Base
days.exercise_description: Walk 5 minutes. Run 90 seconds, walk 60 seconds. Repeat 8 times. Cool down walk. Notice the shift — longer running, shorter walking.
days.coach_tip: Focus on landing midfoot, not on your heels. It reduces impact on your knees significantly.

days.title: First Sustained Run
days.exercise_description: Walk 5 minutes. Run 3 minutes, walk 1 minute. Repeat 6 times. You're now running 18 minutes total. That's more than most people do in a week.
days.coach_tip: Run slower than you think you should. The number one beginner mistake is starting too fast and burning out.

days.title: The 10-Minute Block
days.exercise_description: Walk 5 minutes. Run 10 minutes, walk 3 minutes, run 10 minutes. Cool down. This is a mental milestone — you can sustain effort for 10 minutes straight.
days.coach_tip: When your brain says stop, your body usually has 40% left. Keep going. Trust the training.

days.title: Non-Stop 20 Minutes
days.exercise_description: Walk 5 minutes. Run 20 minutes without stopping. Any pace — just keep your feet moving. Walk 5 minutes. You just ran farther than 80% of adults did today.
days.coach_tip: Don't look at your phone. Don't check the time. Just run. Put on a podcast or playlist and let the time pass.

title: Sunrise Yoga Flow
description: Start your morning with gentle flows that build flexibility and set a calm tone for the day.

days.title: Breath & Awareness
days.exercise_description: 5 minutes of seated breathing (inhale 4 counts, hold 4, exhale 6). Then gentle cat-cow stretches for 3 minutes. Finish with child's pose for 2 minutes. The goal today is simply to arrive on the mat.
days.coach_tip: If your mind wanders during breathing, that's normal. Notice it, name it, and return to the count.

days.title: Sun Salutation A
days.exercise_description: Learn the 12-pose Sun Salutation A sequence. Practice it slowly 3 times with the video, then 3 times on your own. Focus on linking breath to movement — inhale up, exhale fold.
days.coach_tip: Your heels don't need to touch the floor in downward dog. Bend your knees. Flexibility comes with consistency, not force.

days.title: Standing Balance
days.exercise_description: Tree pose, warrior III, and eagle pose. Hold each for 30 seconds per side, 3 rounds. Use a wall for support if needed — there's no shame in stability aids.
days.coach_tip: Fix your gaze on a single point that isn't moving. Balance starts with your eyes, not your feet.

days.title: Hip Openers
days.exercise_description: Pigeon pose, lizard lunge, butterfly stretch, and happy baby. Hold each for 60-90 seconds. Breathe into the tightness — don't fight it.
days.coach_tip: Tight hips affect everything: your back, your knees, even your sleep. This session might be the most important one this week.

days.title: Gentle Backbends
days.exercise_description: Cobra, sphinx, bridge pose, and supported fish pose. These counteract desk-sitting posture. 3 rounds of each, holding for 20-30 seconds.
days.coach_tip: Backbends should open your chest, not compress your lower back. If you feel pinching, reduce the depth.

days.title: Flow & Strength
days.exercise_description: Combine everything into a 20-minute vinyasa flow. Sun salutations → warrior sequence → balance → hip openers → savasana. Move at your own pace.
days.coach_tip: Yoga isn't about looking like the instructor. It's about feeling what your body needs today and responding honestly.

days.title: Restorative & Meditation
days.exercise_description: Supported child's pose (5 min), legs up the wall (5 min), reclined twist (3 min each side), savasana (5 min). Use pillows and blankets generously.
days.coach_tip: This might feel like 'doing nothing.' It's not. Restorative yoga activates your parasympathetic nervous system — it's recovery at a cellular level.

title: Power Yoga for Athletes
description: A challenging yoga program that builds strength, balance, and mobility for active people.

days.title: Warrior Strength Series
days.exercise_description: Warrior I, II, and III held for 45 seconds each side. Chair pose for 60 seconds. Crescent lunge pulses. 3 full rounds. Your legs will burn — that's the point.
days.coach_tip: Sink lower in your warriors. Most athletes go too shallow because they're used to moving, not holding.

days.title: Arm Balance Prep
days.exercise_description: Crow pose progressions, side plank variations, and dolphin pose. Work on shifting your weight forward over your hands. Even if you don't get airborne, the process builds incredible shoulder stability.
days.coach_tip: Put a pillow in front of your face for crow pose attempts. Knowing you won't smash your nose makes you 50% braver.

days.title: Deep Twists & Binds
days.exercise_description: Revolved triangle, bound side angle, seated spinal twists. These wring out tension in your thoracic spine and challenge your balance under rotation.
days.coach_tip: Twist from your mid-back, not your neck. Your head should follow your spine, not lead it.

days.title: Core Ignite
days.exercise_description: Boat pose holds, bicycle crunches in slow motion, plank to downward dog taps, and bird-dog. Controlled movements — no momentum, no shortcuts. 4 sets of 30 seconds each.
days.coach_tip: If your hip flexors are doing all the work in boat pose, bend your knees more. Feel it in your abs, not your quads.

days.title: Full Power Flow
days.exercise_description: A continuous 30-minute flow combining all elements from this week. Sun salutations, warrior series, arm balances, twists, core work, finishing with hip openers and savasana.
days.coach_tip: Set an intention before you start. Not a goal — an intention. Something like 'I move with purpose' or 'I earn my rest.'

title: 7-Minute HIIT Blitz
description: Maximum results in minimum time. Scientific 7-minute workouts that torch calories.

days.title: Classic 7-Minute Circuit
days.exercise_description: 12 exercises, 30 seconds each, 10 seconds rest: jumping jacks, wall sit, push-ups, crunches, step-ups, squats, tricep dips, plank, high knees, lunges, push-up rotation, side plank.
days.coach_tip: 30 seconds feels short until you're in it. Give each exercise everything — you only have 7 minutes.

days.title: Lower Body Burn
days.exercise_description: Squat jumps, wall sits, reverse lunges, calf raises, sumo squats, single-leg deadlifts, lateral lunges, glute bridges, jump lunges, curtsy lunges, squat holds, star jumps.
days.coach_tip: Your quads will scream by exercise 4. That's the lactic acid. Push through it — it clears within 30 seconds of stopping.

days.title: Upper Body Focus
days.exercise_description: Push-ups, tricep dips, plank shoulder taps, diamond push-ups, pike push-ups, arm circles, push-up hold, superman, inchworms, plank up-downs, commando crawl, T push-ups.
days.coach_tip: No equipment doesn't mean no challenge. Slow the tempo down if the movements feel too easy.

days.title: Core Crusher
days.exercise_description: Crunches, bicycle crunches, mountain climbers, plank, leg raises, Russian twists, dead bugs, flutter kicks, sit-ups, plank jacks, V-ups, hollow hold.
days.coach_tip: Press your lower back into the floor during every supine exercise. If it arches, your hip flexors are taking over.

days.title: Cardio Explosion
days.exercise_description: Burpees, tuck jumps, speed skaters, high knees, jumping lunges, star jumps, mountain climbers, lateral hops, squat jumps, burpee broad jumps, sprinter starts, power jacks.
days.coach_tip: Land quietly. Loud landings mean high impact forces on your joints. Absorb each landing like a cat.

days.title: Tabata Finisher
days.exercise_description: Pick your 3 hardest exercises from this week. Tabata each one: 20 seconds max effort, 10 seconds rest, 8 rounds. Rest 1 minute between exercises.
days.coach_tip: Rate your effort honestly: 1-10. If you're not hitting 8+ during work intervals, you're leaving results on the table.

days.title: The Challenge
days.exercise_description: Repeat the Day 1 circuit. But this time, count your reps for each exercise. Write them down. Next week, beat those numbers.
days.coach_tip: Progress isn't always visible in the mirror. But it's always visible in the numbers. Track everything.

title: Lunch Break HIIT
description: 30-minute sessions designed for your lunch break. Shower-friendly intensity that won't leave you drenched.

days.title: Low-Sweat Strength Intervals
days.exercise_description: Tempo squats (4 seconds down, 2 up), push-up holds, isometric lunges, wall sits. 40 seconds work, 20 rest, 6 exercises, 3 rounds. Challenging but controlled — you won't soak through your shirt.
days.coach_tip: Isometric and slow-tempo work builds strength without the sweating that comes from jumping and bouncing.

days.title: Desk Mobility Circuit
days.exercise_description: Seated spinal twists, standing hip circles, doorframe chest stretches, wrist extensions, neck rolls, and ankle alphabet. 30 seconds each, 3 rounds. Counteract 4 hours of sitting.
days.coach_tip: Set a phone alarm every 90 minutes at work. Stand up and do 3 of these movements. Your back will feel 10 years younger.

days.title: Controlled Cardio
days.exercise_description: Marching in place, step-touches, gentle high knees, bodyweight squats, standing oblique crunches. No jumping, no floor work. 12 minutes of continuous movement at 60-70% effort.
days.coach_tip: This is 'zone 2' cardio — you should be able to talk in full sentences. It's the intensity where your body burns fat most efficiently.

days.title: Band Resistance Circuit
days.exercise_description: If you have a resistance band: banded pull-aparts, banded squats, banded bicep curls, banded rows. No band? Use a filled water bottle. 3 sets of 15 for each.
days.coach_tip: Keep a resistance band in your desk drawer. It's the most versatile, portable piece of gym equipment that exists.

days.title: Mindful Movement
days.exercise_description: Slow-motion sun salutations, deep breathing squats, balance work with eyes closed, and a 3-minute body scan meditation. The goal is to return to your afternoon with more focus than caffeine provides.
days.coach_tip: This session is about mental recovery as much as physical. Don't skip it because it seems 'too easy.' Easy and effective aren't opposites.

title: Total Body Flexibility
description: Systematic stretching program that targets every major muscle group. Improve range of motion in 3 weeks.

days.title: Flexibility Assessment
days.exercise_description: Test your current flexibility: toe touch, seated straddle, shoulder reach-behind, quad stretch, hip flexor lunge. Take photos or measure distances. No judgment — just data.
days.coach_tip: Flexibility varies wildly day to day based on stress, sleep, and hydration. Test at the same time of day for consistency.

days.title: Hamstrings & Calves
days.exercise_description: Standing forward fold (2 min), seated pike stretch (2 min), single-leg hamstring stretch (90 sec each), standing calf stretch (60 sec each), downward dog pedaling (2 min).
days.coach_tip: Never bounce in a stretch. Hold steady, breathe out, and let gravity do the work. Your muscles release, they don't get forced open.

days.title: Hips & Glutes
days.exercise_description: Pigeon pose (2 min each side), figure-4 stretch (90 sec each), deep squat hold (2 min), frog stretch (2 min), 90/90 hip switches (10 each direction).
days.coach_tip: If pigeon pose causes knee pain, switch to figure-4 on your back. Same stretch, zero knee stress.

days.title: Shoulders & Chest
days.exercise_description: Doorframe chest stretch (60 sec each), cross-body shoulder stretch (60 sec each), thread-the-needle (90 sec each), overhead tricep stretch, behind-the-back clasp.
days.coach_tip: Most people stretch what's already flexible and ignore what's tight. If a stretch feels impossible, that's exactly the one you need most.

days.title: Spine & Thoracic
days.exercise_description: Cat-cow (2 min), seated spinal twist (90 sec each), foam roller thoracic extension (2 min), child's pose with side reach (60 sec each), supine twist (2 min each).
days.coach_tip: Your thoracic spine (mid-back) is designed to rotate. If it's stiff, your lower back compensates — and that's where pain lives.

days.title: Full Body Flow
days.exercise_description: Combine all stretches into a 25-minute flow. Move from standing to floor: forward fold → lunges → pigeon → seated stretches → twists → supine stretches → savasana.
days.coach_tip: Hold each stretch just past the point of mild discomfort. If you're grimacing, back off. Flexibility grows at the edge, not past it.

days.title: Reassess & Compare
days.exercise_description: Repeat the Day 1 assessment. Compare photos and measurements. Most people gain 1-2 inches on their toe touch in just one week of consistent stretching.
days.coach_tip: Flexibility isn't a talent — it's a practice. The people who can do the splits just stretched more often than you. That's literally it.

title: Office Worker Stretch Routine
description: Targeted stretches for desk workers. Undo the damage of 8 hours of sitting in just 15 minutes.

days.title: Neck & Traps Release
days.exercise_description: Gentle neck tilts (ear to shoulder, 30 sec each), chin tucks (10 reps), upper trap stretch with hand behind back (45 sec each), levator scapulae stretch (45 sec each).
days.coach_tip: If you carry stress in your shoulders, this routine alone can reduce your tension headaches by half. Do it daily.

days.title: Wrists & Forearms
days.exercise_description: Wrist flexor stretch (30 sec each), wrist extensor stretch (30 sec each), prayer stretch, reverse prayer, finger spreads, and wrist circles. 3 rounds of each.
days.coach_tip: If you type all day and never stretch your wrists, you're on a slow path to carpal tunnel. This is preventive medicine.

days.title: Lower Back & Hip Flexors
days.exercise_description: Kneeling hip flexor stretch (90 sec each), supine knee-to-chest (60 sec each), pelvic tilts (15 reps), standing quad stretch (45 sec each), psoas march (10 each).
days.coach_tip: Your hip flexors shorten every minute you sit. After 8 hours, they're pulling your pelvis forward and your lower back into pain.

days.title: Chest & Shoulders (Posture Fix)
days.exercise_description: Wall angels (10 slow reps), doorframe stretch (60 sec), band pull-aparts or towel stretch (15 reps), prone Y-T-W raises (8 each), chin tuck against wall (10 reps).
days.coach_tip: Forward head posture adds 10 pounds of perceived weight to your neck for every inch forward. Wall angels teach your spine where 'straight' actually is.

days.title: Full Desk Worker Reset
days.exercise_description: Combine the best stretch from each day into a single 15-minute routine: neck release → wrist stretches → hip flexor stretch → chest opener → lower back decompression → 2 min savasana.
days.coach_tip: Bookmark this routine. Do it every workday at 3pm. In two weeks, you'll notice less stiffness, better posture, and more energy in the afternoon.
